skill.name: prompt-optimize
skill.description: Prompt engineering skill for improving AI instructions through collaborative review, architecture suggestions, safety guardrails, and polished final prompts.
skill.summary: Optimize prompts with expert structure, clearer constraints, and safer AI behavior.
skill.author: YYH211

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, and high entropy. Review found markdown examples, frontmatter text, and Chinese-language content rather than executable code, cryptographic operations, or obfuscated payloads.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick locations are markdown code fences around sample conversations and scenarios. No shell command, script execution, or user-controlled command construction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review confirms these spans are fenced markdown examples. The file contains no executable Ruby, shell calls, or tool instructions that run commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported line is frontmatter description text for the skill. No hashing, encryption, random number generation, or cryptographic API use appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only metadata prose about prompt engineering. There is no cryptographic operation to evaluate.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The file is plain markdown with substantial Chinese text and examples. No binary blob, encoded payload, packed script, or hidden network indicator was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found readable markdown throughout the file. The entropy signal is consistent with multilingual prose, not obfuscation.

content.value_statement: Poor prompts produce vague, unstable, or unsafe AI responses. This skill guides structured prompt improvement through diagnosis, architecture choices, examples, and safety constraints.
actual_capabilities: Reviews a prompt and identifies unclear goals, missing constraints, and weak output structure.
Suggests prompt architectures such as chain of thought, self-consistency, tree of thoughts, and ReAct.
Guides users through collaborative questions before rewriting important prompts.
Adds safety guardrails for public agents and user-input workflows.
Delivers final prompts in copy-ready Markdown or XML-style structures.
limitations: It does not test prompts against live model outputs by itself.
It depends on the user to provide goals, context, audience, and constraints.
It may suggest advanced prompting patterns that are unnecessary for simple tasks.
It does not replace legal, medical, or compliance review for regulated use cases.

use_cases.title: Improve Assistant Instructions
use_cases.description: Turn rough assistant behavior notes into a clear system prompt with role, scope, constraints, and output format.

use_cases.title: Refine Team Prompts
use_cases.description: Review internal prompts for summaries, research, planning, or writing so teams get more consistent results.

use_cases.title: Harden Public Agents
use_cases.description: Add boundaries, refusal behavior, and instruction isolation for assistants that receive unpredictable user input.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Prompt Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize this prompt for clarity and reliability. Explain the main weaknesses, ask one important question if needed, then provide an improved version: [paste prompt].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Prompt from Goals
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a new prompt from a task description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a prompt for this task: [describe task]. Ask for missing context, recommend a structure, and produce a final prompt after I answer.

prompt_templates.title: Add Safety Guardrails
prompt_templates.scenario: Strengthen a public or user-facing assistant prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this assistant prompt for prompt injection, jailbreak, and unsafe behavior risks. Suggest guardrails and rewrite the prompt with clear boundaries: [paste prompt].

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Architecture Upgrade
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a complex reasoning or creative workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this prompt as a prompt architect. Decide whether chain of thought, self-consistency, tree of thoughts, step-back prompting, or ReAct would improve it. Explain the tradeoffs and produce the upgraded prompt: [paste prompt].

output_examples.input: A user provides a short prompt asking an AI to write product names.
output_examples.output: The skill proposes a two-stage creative workflow: generate varied options first, then evaluate them against brand and audience criteria.

output_examples.input: A team wants meeting notes converted into useful summaries.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a structured summary with key takeaways, action items, owners, and discussion points.

output_examples.input: A developer shares instructions for a customer support chatbot.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies public interaction risks and adds boundaries, refusal behavior, and instruction isolation guidance.

best_practices: Provide the current prompt, target users, expected output, and failure examples.
Confirm architecture choices before asking for a final rewrite.
Include safety requirements when the prompt handles public or sensitive input.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a final prompt without explaining the task and audience.
Do not add complex reasoning frameworks when a simple template is enough.
Do not use generated prompts in production without testing representative inputs.

faq.question: What does this skill optimize?
faq.answer: It improves prompts, system instructions, assistant roles, output formats, and safety constraints.

faq.question: Does it write the final prompt immediately?
faq.answer: It may ask clarifying questions first, especially when the goal or constraints are unclear.

faq.question: Can it help with Claude prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. It is written for Claude-style prompt engineering and also supports Codex and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it include prompt injection guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends instruction isolation, role boundaries, and refusal behavior for risky use cases.

faq.question: Is this a testing framework?
faq.answer: No. It designs and reviews prompts, but users still need to test outputs with real examples.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Use it if you build AI assistants, write reusable prompts, or need more reliable model behavior.
